_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Conduct hardware and/or software static and dynamic analysis to reverse engineer malicious or benign systems.
reference_document_element_description: Implement AI application security controls, including prompt injection defenses, output filtering, access controls, and audit logging.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-002 (TF, cosine: 0.465), SP-T-009 (SP, cosine: 0.455).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.2/10 (raw 0.521). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [analysis, benign, conduct, dynamic, engineer, hardware] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, ai, application, audit, controls, defenses] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.844 (P=0.834, R=0.854). Balanced P/R — the 15-word DCWF element and 16-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (98.7%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Conduct hardware and/or software static and dynamic analysis to reverse engineer" and "Implement AI application security controls, including prompt injection defenses,". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.496 ≈ STS 0.521 (gap 0.026). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [none] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.6%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [analysis, benign, conduct, dynamic, engineer, hardware]. WIDAI terms: [access, ai, application, audit, controls, defenses].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 98.7% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
